title: rhynchospora fascicularis subsp. fascicularis
intro: Rhynchospora fascicularis subsp. fascicularis, also known as Marsh Hair-grass, is a type of perennial sedge plant native to North America, characterized by its slender stems and small, greenish-brown flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Marsh Hair-grass include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support digestive health, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various ailments. Marsh Hair-grass is typically prepared as a tea or infusion, and can also be used in salves or ointments to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
